w:r.w:t: Thành Phố Hồ Chí Minh, ngày 25 tháng 05 năm 2026
Document Code: <Code of the document >– v<x.x>
4.6. On-line User Documentation and Help System Requirements

The purpose of this document is to describe the Software Requirement Specification (SRS) for the Parking Building Management System (PBMS). This system is designed to support the operation and management of parking buildings in urban areas where parking demand is high and parking space is limited.

The system provides functionalities for managing vehicle entry and exit, parking slots, parking sessions, reservations, payments, pricing policies, reports, and system administration.This document is intended for:
The document defines the functional and non-functional requirements of the system and serves as the foundation for system design, implementation, testing, and maintenance.

The Parking Building Management System is a web-based system developed to automate and optimize parking management operations in a building or multi-level parking facility. The system supports the following major features:
Manage parking floors, zones, and parking slots.
Classify parking slots by vehicle type.
Calculate parking duration and parking fees.
Handle lost tickets and incorrect vehicle information.
Allow users to reserve parking slots in advance.
Support cash and online payment simulation.
Advanced AI prediction and machine learning systems.
A parking space for a vehicle
A record of a vehicle parking activity
Booking a parking slot before arrival
Quick Response Code used for parking tickets
Section 1 describes the introduction, scope, definitions, and references.
Section 2 describes the overall system architecture, user roles, assumptions, and constraints.
Section 3 defines the specific functional and non-functional requirements.
Section 4 describes system models, workflows, and use cases.
Section 5 contains appendices and supporting materials.
The Parking Building Management System is an independent web-based application that manages parking operations in multi-floor parking buildings. The system interacts with:
The system can also integrate with:
Camera systems for license plate recognition
Scan or manually input license plates
Manage parking floors and parking zones
Handle parking exceptions (lost ticket, incorrect vehicle information)
Track parking duration and temporary parking fees
Uses the system to view parking information, reserve parking slots, manage personal vehicles, track parking sessions, and make parking payments.
Handles daily parking operations such as vehicle entry and exit registration, parking session management, payment processing, and parking exception handling.
Manages parking buildings, floors, parking slots, pricing policies, operational reports, occupancy monitoring, and parking business activities.
Maintains system configuration, user accounts, role-based access control, security settings, system monitoring, backup, and recovery operations.
Supports intelligent parking operations such as parking slot recommendation, occupancy prediction, parking distribution analysis, and license plate recognition assistance.
Local network support inside parking facilities
The system shall comply with the following constraints:
Role-based access control must be implemented.
Parking slot updates must support real-time synchronization.
The system must support multiple parking floors.
Data consistency must be maintained during vehicle entry and exit.
Parking staff are trained to operate the system.
Vehicle license plates can be manually entered or scanned.
QR code or ticket printing devices
Camera devices for license plate recognition
A parking slot can only contain one active parking session at a time.
Vehicles must be assigned to compatible parking zones.
Parking fees are calculated based on pricing policies.
Vehicles cannot exit before payment confirmation.
Reserved slots cannot be assigned to other vehicles during reservation time.
Lost ticket cases require staff verification.
The driver arrives at the parking entrance.
Staff scans or enters vehicle information.
The system allocates suitable parking slots.
The driver receives a ticket or QR code.
The driver submits a ticket or QR code.
The system retrieves the parking session.
Slot status is updated to available.
The vehicle exits the parking building.
The driver selects the reservation time.
Describes the process where a logged-in driver selects and reserves an available parking slot in the building via the mobile application before arriving.
To successfully reserve a parking slot and change its real-time status from available (Green) to pending (Yellow).
The driver taps on the "Make Reservation" feature on the mobile application dashboard.
The driver has successfully registered an account and logged into the system
The parking building has at least one empty parking slot available.
The selected parking slot status is updated to pending (Yellow).
The system records the reservation details, including the vehicle's license plate and the estimated arrival time.
The system sends a reservation confirmation notification to the driver.
<List the main steps for using this case to reach the goal successfully >
The driver accesses the real-time parking layout screen.
The system displays a visual map of all parking slots (available slots are highlighted in
The driver selects an empty slot marked in
The system displays the reservation form requiring input details.
The system validates the entered data format.
The system saves the reservation information into the database.
The system updates the selected slot's status from "Available" (
The system displays a success message: "Reservation Completed Successfully!".
3a. The driver wants to find a slot near a specific zone (e.g., elevator/exit):
3a1. The driver uses the "Filter/Search" tool on the layout screen to choose preferred zones.
3a2. The system filters and highlights only the matching available slots in Green.
3a3. The flow returns to Step 3 of the Main Success Scenario.
5a. The driver enters an invalid license plate format or an estimated arrival time in the past:
5a1. The system blocks the submission and displays a specific validation error message (e.g., "Invalid arrival time format" or "License plate field cannot be empty").
5a2. The system keeps the form open for the driver to re-enter valid data.
: Login (The driver must be authenticated before performing this action).
Parking slots must strictly adhere to real-time color representation:
A pending slot (Yellow) will only be held for a maximum of
past the "Estimated Arrival Time" entered by the driver. If the vehicle fails to check in at the gate by then, the slot automatically reverts to
Each registered driver account is restricted to a maximum of
at any given time to prevent system hoarding.
Describes the process where a new user creates an account on the system using either their Email/Password or their existing Google account.
To successfully create a new user account in the system database, allowing the user to log in and use premium features like booking parking slots. .
The driver taps on the "Make Reservation" feature on the mobile application dashboard. Or clicks on the "Register" or "Sign Up" button on the interface.
The user is currently an unauthenticated guest and has access to the application/website.
A new account record is created and stored in the database.
The user is automatically logged into the system.
The user is redirected to the home dashboard.
<List the main steps for using this case to reach the goal successfully >
The system displays a rectangular modal/popup containing registration options:
- Method 1: Email and Password input fields.
The user fills in their email address and a secure password, then clicks the "
The system validates the email format and password strength.
The system checks if the email already exists in the database.
The system encrypts the password and saves the new account data into the database.
The system automatically logs the user in and displays a success notification: "Registration Successful!".
3a. The user chooses to register via Method 2 ("Continue with Google"):
3a1. The user clicks the "Continue with Google" button.
3a2. The system redirects the user to the Google Authentication secure login page.
3a3. The user signs in with their Google credentials and grants permission to the app.
3a4. Google returns a secure authentication token along with the user's basic profile info (Email, Name).
3a5. The system checks if this Google email is already registered. If not, it creates a new account automatically.
3a6. The flow proceeds to Step 7 of the Main Success Scenario.
If the user enters an invalid email format or a weak password:
The system blocks the submission and highlights the specific field with an error message (e.g., "Invalid email address" or "Password must be at least 8 characters long").
The system keeps the rectangular registration modal open for re-entry.
If the entered email already exists in the system database:
The system displays an error message: "This email is already registered. Please log in or use another email."
If the Google Authentication process fails or is canceled by the user:
The system cancels the registration loop, displays an alert: "Google Sign-In canceled," and returns the user back to the rectangular registration modal.
(The registration form usually provides a direct link to switch to the Login modal if the user realizes they already have an account).
All passwords registered via Method 1 must be strictly hashed using a secure encryption algorithm (e.g., BCrypt) before being saved into the database; plain text passwords must never be stored.
Every account in the system must be mapped to a unique email address. Duplicate emails are strictly forbidden.
When using "Continue with Google," the system must trust Google's verification status and skip the traditional email verification code step.
Parking Staff: Maximum 30 minutes of instruction to master check-in, manual license plate entry, and camera reconciliation.
Manager: Maximum 1 hour to become familiar with the pricing configuration interface and viewing statistical reports.
Driver: need a period of time using the website to become familiar with it and understand how to perform operations.
The staff shall be able to manually enter an incorrect license plate within 30 seconds.
Reconciling entry/exit images and confirming payment at the exit gate: No more than 20 seconds.
Web Interface (Staff, Manager): Must display correctly on desktops and tablets (Responsive).
Web (Driver): Follows basic design rules; buttons are large and clear; supports Dark Mode for night use.
The system operates continuously 24/7 and must ensure stable operation under normal usage conditions.
Maintenance (if needed): Performed during off-peak hours (1:00 AM – 3:00 AM) and must be announced in advance on the App.
When the internet is lost, the system on Staff workstations automatically switches to Offline Mode: allows taking vehicle photos, storing information locally.
When connectivity is restored, data is automatically synchronized to the Server within 5 minutes.
Parking fee calculation must be 100% correct according to the configured time blocks.
Critical Bug: Must not cause system crash or loss of revenue/historical data. Must be fixed within 45 minutes.
Minor Bug: Display or UI glitches that do not affect core functions. Fixed in the next update.
API showing available slots to the Driver App: < 500ms.
AI license plate recognition (from image capture to text result): < 2 seconds.
Revenue and peak-hour report charts: Load within < 3 seconds.
Handles vehicle flow with a minimum throughput of 10 vehicles/minute during peak hours.
Stores history of at least 200,000 vehicle entries with associated images without performance degradation.
Comments are required for complex functions (progressive pricing, AI position suggestion).
Logs are retained for at least 1 month for audit purposes.
On-line User Documentation and Help System Requirements
Web (Staff/Manager): Includes a "User Guide" page with illustrated steps for vehicle entry/exit handling and lost ticket cases.
Mobile App (Driver): Includes a "Help & FAQs" section answering questions about booking cancellation and payment wallet linking.
License Plate Recognition: Open-source library (e.g., Tesseract OCR) or third
party API; must support Vietnamese motorcycle and car license plates.
Payment: Official SDKs provided by MoMo, ZaloPay, VNPay.
Staff: Displays entry and exit images side
side on the same screen. Manual license plate entry button is large and clear.
Driver: Minimalist design focused on the parking map, available slot count, and a "Quick Pay" button.
IP Camera: Connects via RTSP protocol.
LED Display: Connected to show available slot data.
Payment Gateway: Integrates with VNPay, MoMo, ZaloPay APIs via Webhook.
Internal: Floor management module communicates with the AI suggestion module to update slot coordinates.
All data transmission between App/Web and Server: HTTPS.
Real-time available slot updates: Auto refresh every 5 seconds.
free libraries. Only costs incurred are for Cloud server and payment transaction fees.
Display copyright notice at the bottom of the Web interface and in the App’s "About" section.
The App must include a Privacy Policy stating that vehicle plate images and customer photos are used only internally for parking security purposes and are not shared externally.
Data Format: License plates stored in the database must follow Vietnamese standard license plate format (e.g., 36A-XXXXX or 36F1-XXXXX).
Payment Security: Payment API calls must comply with the technical specifications of the e
Automated system engineered to capture and extract vehicle registration text via computational imaging modules (Section 1.2, 2.2).
Regulatory rendering interface standard classifying slot vacancy: Green (Available), Yellow (Pending Reservation), and Red (Occupied/In-use).
Fallback automated state switching workstation instances to local data synchronization structures when local network connectivity fails (Section 4.2).
A temporary system locking an empty parking asset from general inventory allocation upon initial reservation success.
Explicit business constraint limiting the validity of a pending reservation slot holding to 30 minutes past estimated arrival benchmarks.
The following sequence visualizes the step-by-step interaction flow between the
and the system for UC001: Make Reservation:
To satisfy the non-functional requirements for
(responsive layouts, clear target touch-points, and high-contrast visuals), the user interfaces for the mobile application module are specified below:
Allows drivers to view asset vacancy and initialize the booking workflow.
High-contrast grids, support for system Dark Mode, and target touch areas 44 x 44 dp.
Captures targeted metadata required to secure the parking transaction.
Automatically initialized upon selecting any validation-passed
Render real-time visual parking layout map.
Capture vehicle license plate and estimated arrival.
BR03 (Enforce maximum of 1 active reservation per driver).
Transition slot state and hold reservation temporally.
BR02 (Execute automatic cancellation after 30-minute grace period).
Perform client-side validation on structural data inputs.
Exception 5a (Block data submissions with historical timestamps).
